plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero for capital preservation.
Exit QQQI and SMH due to downtrends and sell signals.
Reduce QQQ exposure as it is flagged for sale.
Add a small position in MSFU, a high-scoring quality setup.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and a large cash buffer.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation and significantly reduced drawdown risk. It aims for steady returns by avoiding high-beta assets and maintaining a substantial cash buffer.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to reduce overall leverage.
Exit QQQI and SMH due to downtrends and sell signals.
Reduce QQQ exposure as it is flagged for sale.
Add MSFU and DDOG, both high-scoring quality setups, for growth.
This plan balances growth with risk management, maintaining a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, aiming for risk-first growth with a blend of broad index and quality tech. It seeks to reduce overall leverage and concentration while still capturing upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Exit QQQI and SMH due to downtrends and sell signals.
Reduce QQQ exposure as it is flagged for sale.
Add MSFU and DDOG, high-scoring setups, to press what is working.
Maintain existing SOXL and SPAL as part of the leveraged ETF allocation.
Consider adding to SOXL if its trend improves and it clears its stop.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretched growth with higher leverage and concentration in high-scoring setups. Be aware that this mode significantly increases both upside and drawdown potential, and leveraged ETFs experience daily decay/rebalancing. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage the higher risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free capital for income.
Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero and exit high-beta CRWD.
Exit SMH and QQQ to reduce growth exposure and downtrending assets.
Significantly increase QQQI allocation for steady monthly distributions.
This plan prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield through income/covered-call ETFs. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth, but be aware that distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff. This is not a savings account.
